creators.name: Nick Saraev
creators.blurb: Practical AI automation and agency builds from someone shipping real systems.

videos.title: I Built a $1M/y SaaS with Claude Code, Here's How
videos.summary: Nick Saraev describes building Clairvo, a $1M/year SaaS that uses Claude Code-generated techniques like predictive pacing and local number rotation to improve call pickup rates. He argues that using Claude to enumerate opportunities and practical implementation of AI agents is viable for building real-world revenue-generating businesses, contrasting his approach with many who talk automation without results.

videos.title: How to Build Mobile Apps with Claude Code: Full Course (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev's full-length course teaches mobile app development using Claude Code and Expo/React Native. The course covers setup, app design frameworks, building three complete apps (habit tracker, calorie tracker with AI, Pomodoro timer), database integration with Supabase, user authentication, and app store submission.

videos.title: How to Use Claude Code for FREE (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev demonstrates how to use Claude Code for free or at minimal cost by routing through NVIDIA-NIM, OpenRouter, Ollama for local models, and alternatives like DeepSeek V4 Flash. The video includes setup instructions for each method and references a full 4-hour Claude Code course.

videos.title: Claude Opus-4.7 Just Dropped, And...
videos.summary: Claude Opus-4.7 is the latest version of Anthropic's Claude model. Nick Saraev discusses the release and its applications in a YouTube video covering practical automation workflows and business use cases.

creators.name: AI Engineer
creators.blurb: Talks and sessions from the AI Engineer conference and community.

videos.title: 6 Things to Know about AIE World's Fair 2026
videos.summary: The AI Engineer channel announces AIE World's Fair 2026 in San Francisco, highlighting expanded content, expo floor experiences, research-industry synergy, leadership tracks, and vertical-specific sessions. The event features $40k in sponsor offers and limited early-bird ticket availability.

videos.title: The Production AI Playbook: Deploying Agents at Enterprise Scale — Sandipan Bhaumik, Databricks
videos.summary: Sandipan Bhaumik from Databricks presents a five-pillar production framework for enterprise AI agents: evaluation, observability, data foundation, multi-agent orchestration, and governance. The talk uses a retail banking case study where initial PoC failure was traced to stale embeddings after policy updates, demonstrating the critical need for comprehensive tracing and monitoring systems in production AI systems.

videos.title: Your Agent's Biggest Lie: "I Searched the Web" — Rafael Levi, Bright Data
videos.summary: Agents often fail to search the web due to blocks, CAPTCHAs, or stale data but return answers as if successful. Using Bright Data's Web MCP enables real web access, improving reliability, citations, real-time results, and reducing hallucinations compared to agents without proper web connectivity.

videos.title: You Might Not Need 50 Diffusion Steps — Ziv Ilan, Nvidia
videos.summary: NVIDIA's approach combines dynamic quantization, latent caching, and step distillation to reduce diffusion model denoising from fifty steps to one to eight steps, achieving real-time video generation on Blackwell B200. The techniques are modular and composable, with trajectory-based and distribution-based distillation approaches for training student models.

creators.name: Cole Medin
creators.blurb: Practical AI agent and coding-assistant builds, end to end.

videos.title: The Creators of Claude Code and OpenClaw don't Prompt Their Agents Anymore?!
videos.summary: Top AI engineers use automated loops to repeatedly prompt agents instead of manual prompting. An orchestrator agent spins up worker agents in parallel, with the system storing all runs in a database and providing observability dashboards. This approach is more cost-effective than relying solely on expensive frontier models.

videos.title: Omnigent: The New Meta-Harness for EVERY Coding Agent - Claude Code, Codex, Pi, More
videos.summary: Omnigent is a meta-harness that orchestrates multiple coding agents (Claude Code, Codex, Pi) as interchangeable workers. It enables routing tasks to different models based on suitability, includes human-in-the-loop governance, and maintains sessions across devices. The framework is open source and designed to help engineers use the right model for each job.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 is Now BANNED?!
videos.summary: Cole Medin discusses the sudden removal of Claude Fable 5 following a US government export-control directive reportedly triggered by claimed jailbreak vulnerability. He analyzes why Anthropic pulled the model from all customers overnight and shares benchmarking results comparing Fable 5 to Opus 4.8 before its removal.

videos.title: Google's Agents CLI: The CLI + Skills Combination to Ship AI Agents EASILY
videos.summary: Google's Agents CLI is an open source command-line tool for building and deploying AI agents to production. It provides sandbox isolation for agent code execution, audit trails, and integrates with AI coding assistants like Claude.

creators.name: AI Jason
creators.blurb: AI app and agent builds, experiments, and walkthroughs.

videos.title: wtf is Loop Engineer & how to setup for real
videos.summary: AI Jason explains Loop Engineer, a technique for setting up AI agents. The video includes a free course on building AI agents, links to a GitHub template, and a workshop for learning the approach in practical context.

videos.title: How to build proactive agents & self-improving company (Fully explained)
videos.summary: Video explains how to construct proactive AI agents and self-improving systems. Covers building autonomous feedback loops and demonstrates use cases including autonomous ads optimization and SEO. Mentions Loopany framework and the role of memory and tools in enabling autonomous agent capabilities.

videos.title: Ralph-loop 2.0? The real autonomous coder is coming...
videos.summary: AI Jason discusses the /goal feature and related autonomous coding capabilities, comparing emerging systems to Ralph-loop 2.0. He covers goal-setting practices, mission exploration for long-horizon tasks, and tools like Crewlet and Superdesign for building autonomous agents.

videos.title: New AI coding paradiagm - OpenAI Symphony
videos.summary: OpenAI Symphony is a new AI coding paradigm demonstrated by AI Jason that includes codebase harness setup, auto-testing capabilities, and workflow configuration. The presentation covers Symphony's functionality, implementation details, and integration with development practices.

creators.name: Caleb Writes Code
creators.blurb: Hands-on coding sessions and software build-alongs.

videos.title: Karpathy Bigram explained in 10min..
videos.summary: Caleb Writes Code presents Andrej Karpathy's explanation of bigram language models as a foundation for understanding larger language models like GPT. The video covers essential components including tokenization, embeddings, loss functions, softmax, and optimization with AdamW.

videos.title: NVIDIA’s Nemotron 3 Is... Awesome?
videos.summary: NVIDIA released Nemotron 3 models in three sizes: Nano, Super, and Ultra. The series combines hybrid mamba transformer architecture, Mixture of Experts (Latent MoE), and MTP techniques. The models are positioned as open models with distinctive architectural choices compared to competitors.

videos.title: MiniMax M3 explained in 8min..
videos.summary: MiniMax M3 introduces MiniMax Sparse Attention, replacing full attention with sparse attention patterns. The model includes tiled and I/O optimizations for KV cache reads, reducing overhead in both prefill and decode stages of inference.

videos.title: Pi Agent explained in 6min..
videos.summary: Pi is an agent framework highlighted for its unique approach to harnessing, enabling diverse use cases like OpenClaw. The video compares Pi against other frontier agent systems including Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, and Antigravity in terms of complexity and capability.

creators.name: AWS Developers
creators.blurb: Official AWS tutorials, service deep dives, and developer demos.

videos.title: Agent Toolkit for AWS Explained in 2 Minutes
videos.summary: Agent Toolkit for AWS is a framework that equips AI coding agents with tools, documentation, best practices, and guardrails for working with AWS services. It provides access to up-to-date AWS knowledge and procedures to improve agent success rates and reduce inefficient attempts.

videos.title: Running Claude Code on Amazon Bedrock
videos.summary: AWS Bedrock enables developers to run Claude Code with data residency compliance and cost control. The service includes cross-region inference, prompt caching, the Mantle endpoint for efficient routing, per-team cost tracking, and Guardrails for content governance.

videos.title: AI and the Job Market in 2026
videos.summary: AWS Developers discusses how AI is changing tech recruitment and job hunting in 2026, emphasizing that networking and personal branding matter more than mass applications. The content argues that junior developers remain valuable, drawing parallels to historical technology transitions where jobs evolve rather than disappear.

videos.title: 5 Tips for Deploying AI Agents to Production
videos.summary: AWS Developers outlines five production deployment practices for TypeScript AI agents: streaming tool call events for UI responsiveness, layered authentication with API Gateway and Cognito, contracted database access with tenant isolation, guardrails with cost budgets and model routing, and observability via metrics and distributed tracing. The patterns are demonstrated using Strands Agents SDK on AWS infrastructure.

creators.name: Visual AI
creators.blurb: Visual walkthroughs and demos across modern AI tools.

creators.name: Alberta Tech
creators.blurb: Developer tutorials and practical builds in AI and software.

videos.title: Do Google engineers actually vibe code?
videos.summary: A video discussing the prevalence and definition of "vibe coding" among engineers, comparing OpenAI Codex and Claude Code capabilities, and examining vibe coding adoption at Google across 2024-2026. The content explores whether major tech companies and their engineers use informal or intuitive coding practices.

videos.title: This company killed open source
videos.summary: Alberta Tech discusses how a company is affecting the open source landscape, featuring an interview with Mike about Malus. The video examines tensions between open source maintainers and commercial interests, questioning whether this represents an existential threat to open source.

videos.title: I let Claude Code take my job (it did better than me)
videos.summary: Alberta Tech conducted an experiment comparing Claude Code's software development performance against their own work and found the AI outperformed them. The video explores whether AI tools like Claude Code will replace human developers across all job functions.

creators.name: EO
creators.blurb: Conversations and stories with founders and builders in tech.

videos.title: How Gong Built a $7B+ Company in the Age of Vibe Coding | Amit Bendov
videos.summary: Amit Bendov, CEO of Gong, argues that code alone does not create lasting software advantage. Real moats come from customer context, proprietary data, product experience, and operational scale. He contends that fears of AI destroying SaaS companies are exaggerated and that the best AI products build deep customer loyalty through raving fan experiences.

videos.title: "ChatGPT Moment" for Robotics Is Coming. The Real Problem Isn't Intelligence | Stanford, Catie Cuan
videos.summary: Catie Cuan argues that a "ChatGPT Moment" is coming for robotics but that humanoid robot development may be myopic. She contends that the real challenge is not intelligence but designing robots for delight and human interaction rather than utility alone, and that billions of robots will soon enter society requiring thoughtful design philosophy.

videos.title: How to Steal Customers From Giants 600x Bigger than Your Startup | Pigment, Eléonore Crespo
videos.summary: Pigment is an AI performance management platform that integrates finance, supply chain, HR, and sales data for business decision-making. The company has raised over $400 million, grows 2x year-over-year, and counts major firms like Figma, Brex, and Anthropic as customers.

videos.title: The Fastest Way to Know if Your Product Market Fit Is Real | Serval CEO, Jake Stauch
videos.summary: Jake Stauch, CEO of Serval, argues that passionate early users do not signal real product-market fit and contrasts "pull" demand (customers asking pricing) with "push" (keeping customers posted). He advises against formal customer interviews in favor of embedded relationships and emphasizes betting on the trajectory of AI models rather than their current state.

creators.name: Cyril Imhof
creators.blurb: AI automation and workflow builds for real projects.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 with Harness Engineering & Loops Will Do Everything.
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof covers Claude Fable 5 and discusses harness engineering, AI loops, and their applications in regulated industries. The video explores tensions between open and closed source AI models, risks of model shutdowns, and the state of AI-powered SaaS, drawing on experience since 2023 in the space.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 Now Banned in Europe by US Government (EU-Breakdown)
videos.summary: A video by Cyril Imhof discusses the claimed ban of Claude Fable 5 in Europe by the US government. The video references regulatory developments concerning Anthropic's model access across EU jurisdictions.

videos.title: The Ultimate Second Brain for AI Entrepreneurs: Fable 5 Claude with Loops
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof discusses Fable 5, an Anthropic model designed for AI entrepreneurs, highlighting its features, guardrails, memory capabilities, and agentic payment integration compared to Mythos 5.

videos.title: Anthropic's Brand New AI Just Started the Gold Rush (Fable 5)
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof discusses Anthropic's new Claude Fable 5 model and its applications in regulated industries. The video covers new use cases for the model and introduces "Know Your Agent" methodology for effective context engineering in long-running AI applications.

creators.name: PY
creators.blurb: Software engineering, tooling, and developer-workflow deep dives.
